explanation: The rule relies on the selection_cli block which looks for the exact strings '/user:' and '/passwd:'. However, dsacls.exe supports short-form parameter aliases. Using '/U:' instead of '/user:' and '/P:' instead of '/passwd:' achieves the same functionality while failing the string match requirements of the rule.

The Windows command line processor allows the use of double quotes within arguments. Inserting quotes in the middle of a parameter name (e.g., '/pass""wd:') is often ignored by the application's argument parser but prevents simple string-matching detection engines from seeing the forbidden keyword.

By utilizing environment variables to store the sensitive CLI flags, the actual command line logged by many process creation events may show the variable reference (if captured at the right moment) or the rule might fail if it only inspects the initial process call before variable expansion, depending on the specific EDR/SIEM telemetry implementation.
